passage.title: Daily Plan Chart
passage.text: Our group keeps a schedule board under the clock. During the first break, Noah turns it before the first bell. The schedule board has color labels for each part of the school day for the day's job. Our teacher asks us to check the next job and read it aloud. Sometimes we compare today's picture with yesterday's picture and notice one small change. We like the schedule board because it helps us remember the day faster.

questions.prompt: What is this passage mostly about?
questions.rationale: The whole passage explains how the schedule board is used.
The first sentence says the schedule board is under the clock.

questions.prompt: When does Noah use the schedule board?
questions.rationale: The second sentence says Noah uses it during the first break.

questions.prompt: What does the schedule board have?
questions.rationale: The third sentence says the schedule board has color labels.

questions.prompt: What does compare mean in the fifth sentence?
questions.rationale: Compare means to look at two things and think about them together.

questions.prompt: What does notice mean in the fifth sentence?
questions.rationale: Notice means to see and pay attention to something.
